notes: v1.14.36 — globe authenticity pass (brief: PHANTOM-globe-authenticity), three surgical data-array swaps, no markup/CSS. SWAP 1: regenerated the baked NIGHT_B64 night-lights luminance map (360x180, 4-bit, high-nibble-first, row0=north/col0=-180) from ~260 real world-metro coordinates, replacing the coarse old approximation that piled North America's lights too far north with no West-Coast counterweight and no recognizable Texas. Same 43,200-char / 32,400-byte format, byte-aligned in-place splice (file byte-delta 0). The SPECTRUM city-dot seeding loop reads it unchanged, so lights now follow reality: bicoastal US + dark Plains gap + Texas triangle + Florida, Europe's blue banana, India, East-Asian seaboard, Japan/Korea, Brazil coast, Nile, etc. SWAP 2+3: replaced the 10-node hub list with CoreWeave's real current NA+EU footprint (15 named sites: Austin home + Plano, West-TX/Project-Horizon[APPROX], Muskogee OK, Chicago, Lancaster PA, Weehawken, Chester VA, Douglasville, Las Vegas, Reno, Hillsboro, London/Crawley, Barcelona, Glasgow/Eurocentral) and grew ARCS to 18 (14 Austin spokes + 4 backbone: NJ->London, VA->London, London->Barcelona, London->Glasgow); packets extend automatically. Austin stays index 0 (warp target). node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.36, cache bumped, no CSS (block-0 baseline 2760 unaffected). HONEST FLAGS for John: West-TX node (idx 2) coords are approximate (Project Horizon's exact town isn't public — drop the line + its [0,2] arc if you want only precisely-located points); 18 arcs may read busy vs 'subtle' — trim spokes on device if so. DEVICE-GATE (iPhone): NA reads bright East Coast + Great Lakes, dark Plains, bright West Coast, visible Texas triangle + Florida; Europe densest; nodes on real sites; no OLED pile-to-white; FPS holds.
